svg.text: REGULATORY ANALYSIS — GENIUS + CLARITY: THE COMPLETE US CRYPTO AML FRAMEWORK
Case AML-IR-2026-011 · GENIUS Act (signed Jul 2025) · CLARITY Act (pending) · Senate Banking May 14, 2026 · DeFi Title · SEC/CFTC
ACAMS · FATF · BSA · FinCEN · CFTC · SEC
Two-Pillar Framework — AML Implications for Digital Asset Practitioners

g.text: Same gap as Tornado Cash 5th Cir.
Stablecoins (PPSI) → FinCEN + OCC · Full BSA AML · Monthly attestation · SAR · Freeze capability
Digital commodities (Bitcoin/ETH) → CFTC + FinCEN · BSA program · KYC · SAR · Pending rulemaking
Investment contracts (tokens) → SEC + FinCEN · Existing securities AML · Howey test applies
DeFi intermediaries → BSA · AML program · Examination by relevant regulator
Crypto ATMs → Registration · Consumer protection · Compliance officer · Holding periods
Practitioner rule: apply conservative BSA standard to ALL during transition period

svg.text: How CLARITY Closes Gaps from This Research Series

g.text: AML-IR-2026-004 (Tornado Cash): Frontend operators → BSA obligations. Code still immutable — access layer regulated.
AML-IR-2026-006 (Bybit): THORChain routing intermediaries → BSA. Protocol permissionless — access layer = chokepoint.
AML-IR-2026-007 (Brazil P2P): CLARITY DeFi model = international precedent for PL 5256/2025 approach.
AML-IR-2026-010 (GENIUS): GENIUS + CLARITY = complete US framework. Stablecoins + everything else covered.
DeFi title = regulatory answer to the core question of this series: "Who is responsible for AML in decentralized systems?"
Answer: The access layer — frontends, routers, aggregators — not the protocol itself.
Document asset type in every risk
2. DeFi access layer = new AML frontier.
Include access layer analysis in all

svg.text: Author: Phelipe Agnelli — AML & Blockchain Forensics · Sources: CLARITY Act · GENIUS Act · Senate Banking Committee · FinCEN/OFAC · Davis Wright Tremaine · K&L Gates · AML-IR-2026-011
